learning_outcomes.description: demonstrate In-depth Knowledge of most of the forms, grammar and syntax of the Latin language
demonstrate Effective Communication Skills through completion of the written assignments and through oral translation in class
demonstrate Independence and Critical Judgement in interpreting and translating the graded reading passage
demonstrate Ethical and Social Understanding of the social and cultural values of the Roman world as expressed through its language and literature

assessments.task_description:
PART 1: ASSIGNMENTS


Copies of all Assignments, and their due dates, are on the Blackboard Site for LATN2120.

REQUIREMENTS : There are five (5) Assignments in total, designed to increase your skills in translation into English and/or Latin, comprehension, vocab recognition, parsing, grammar and syntax analysis.

  • All five (5) Assignments must be completed (with genuine attempts for ALL questions as instructed) in order to obtain a passing grade for the course
  • Uploading a document without answer(s) does not constitute a genuine submission.
  • Each Assignment is worth 20%. Your final mark will be calculated from the sum of these five assignments divided by 5.
  • Assignments must be submitted typed and in word format via Blackboard by the due time and date.


Academic Misconduct: AI, Machine Translation and Referencing

Unacknowledged use of Machine Translation and/or AI to complete assessment or unapproved learning tasks may be referred for academic misconduct. If you use AI or Machine Translation to complete assignments, you must both acknowledge this use and explain how and why AI and/or Machine Translation was used.

Use of Machine Translation and/or AI to complete assessment tasks while language learning skills are still being developed is likely to result in students failing to meet core learning outcomes for the course. Students relying on Machine Translation and AI to complete assessment are also more likely to fail in-person and other assessment tasks.


Part 2: Select Option A or B: These assessment items are designed to help you to acquire vocabulary and to master declension and conjugation patterns in particular

Option A uqlatin Extend QUIZZES 15%

EITHER 972 or more uqlatin quizzes (located on UQ Extend) completed throughout semester or 2 timed Blackboard quizzes held in conjunction with the mid-semester and final exam


uqlatin quizzes:

There are 324 quizzes in total. Students must complete 3 genuine attempts at all the following types of quizzes on uqlatin Extend, with their overall number of quizzes attempted equalling 972:

  • noun declensions
  • adjective/pronoun declension
  • verb conjugation
  • word matching/vocab translation
  • translation/identification of prepositions and idiomatic constructions


If you elect to take this option, you must start your quiz practice in Week 1, or you will not reach the required number of quizzes by the end of semester.

  • Proper attempts must be made for each question (e.g. a genuine answer must be entered for every question).
  • However, students are not required to get all answers correct to receive the full 15%. The purpose of this assessment task is to make learning vocab meaning, conjugation & declension patterns etc part of your automatic recall/memory.



STUDENTS WHO DO NOT TAKE UP THIS OPTION MUST COMPLETE OPTION B: BLACKBOARD TIMED QUIZZES

Two (2) timed Blackboard Quizzes held in conjunction with the mid-semester and final exams

Each quiz is 10 mins long, and only one attempt is permitted for each quiz. The mark for each quiz will be added together and divided by 2 to calculate the final mark out of 15. Quizzes will cover:

  • noun declensions
  • adjective/pronoun declensions
  • noun/adjective/pronoun/participle matching
  • verb conjugations
  • person and number, verb principal parts, mood tense and voice
  • vocab meaning
  • translation of short phrases


Please prepare for these tests by completing some of the UQ Extend quizzes, Blackboard In Class Tests, revising by handwriting, with flashcards, learning vocabulary etc.


Details of both assessment tasks are available on Blackboard
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assignments submission links are on Blackboard. All assignments must be submitted through the Turnitin link on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
9/09/2026 8:00 am

The Mid-Semester Exam will be held in person, in-class. Please remember to bring your student ID card
assessments.task_description:
The mid-semester exam will consist of a variety of translation, parsing, and grammatical questions based on Sections 2D-3D of the Text and the Grammar Book from POD, plus accumulated knowledge from LATN1110. The assignment will test your ability to:

  • translate the set texts taken from Sections 2D-3D into good readable English demonstrating that you understand the Latin
  • translate unseen sentences and/or passages from Latin into idiomatic English based on learning vocabulary from Semester 1 in LATN1110 and Sections 2D-3D covered in LATN2120.
  • translate sentences from English into Latin based on the learning vocabulary as above
  • to parse and to identify grammatical constructions and other syntax accurately via questions and exercises

 

please ensure that you download the vocab pdf at the beginning of semester and refer to it weekly.


Unacknowledged use of Machine Translation and/or AI to complete assessment or unapproved learning tasks may be referred for academic misconduct.

Use of Machine Translation and/or AI to complete assessment tasks while language learning skills are still being developed is likely to result in students failing to meet core learning outcomes for the course. Students relying on Machine Translation and AI to complete assessment are also more likely to fail in-person assessment tasks.

Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Question papers and exam booklets will be collected by the invigilator at the end of the exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
The Final Exam (in -person) consists of a variety of translation, parsing, and grammatical questions based on Sections 2D-4Ei of the Text and the Grammar Book from POD, plus accumulated knowledge from LATN1110. The test will test your ability to:

  • translate the set texts taken from Sections 4A-4E(i) into good readable English demonstrating that you understand the Latin
  • translate unseen sentences, phrases, constructions, sentences and/or passage(s) from Latin into idiomatic English. The sentences will be based on learning vocabulary from Semester 1 in LATN1110 and Sections 2D-4E(i) covered in LATN2120: please ensure that you download the vocab pdf at the beginning of semester and refer to it weekly. Other vocab will be provided.
  • parse words and phrases, identify grammatical constructions and other syntax accurately in the texts set for translation and in other questions


Unacknowledged use of Machine Translation and/or AI to complete assessment or unapproved learning tasks may be referred for academic misconduct.

Use of Machine Translation and/or AI to complete assessment tasks while language learning skills are still being developed is likely to result in students failing to meet core learning outcomes for the course. Students relying on Machine Translation and AI to complete assessment are also more likely to fail in-person assessment tasks.

Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.assessment_title: Skill Building: Class Participation and Practice
assessments.task_description:
Students are required to translate set texts assigned before class, as listed by Section in the Course Profile and on Blackboard. These texts will be re translated and discussed in class in conjunction with points of grammar, syntax, literary style, and where relevant, historical and/or cultural context. A selection will also be re-examined in the mid-semester and final exam.

Each student will also be assigned a specific set of lines to translate individually in class, which will be assigned weekly throughout the semester.

Points of grammar, syntax, and vocabulary acquisition will also be reinforced though class exercises which are designed to provide immediate feedback, focus on points of grammar and syntax, literary technique, and relevant historical and/or cultural contexts. Students are encouraged to use the class time to ask questions encountered during preparation or that arise in class during translation, discussion, or during the completion of class exercises.

Students are encouraged to use the specific Learning Materials that have been developed for this course, which will be explained in Week 1 of the course.

Although AI and Machine translation can be used for this task, at beginner level it does not help students to master the skills needed, and in some cases may be misleading and potentially more time-consuming. Nor will foundational skills be mastered by regularly writing down the translations from Blackboard and reading them out without attempting to translate and understand the Latin texts yourself.

Use of Machine Translation and/or AI to complete assessment tasks while language learning skills are still being developed is likely to result in students failing to meet core learning outcomes for the course. Students relying on Machine Translation and AI to complete assessment are also more likely to fail in-person assessment tasks.

Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students are required to be in class to translate their set lines: written work cannot be submitted in lieu of in-person attendance. Please print out your translated lines and hand them in at the beginning of class. While these will not be assigned an individual mark as this exercise is about providing feedback through discussion, they form part of your overall participation mark. In person class participation is demonstrated through active participation in all components of the class: demonstration of prepared translations of set texts for weekly classes on Wednesday and Friday, in-class translation and discussion of set lines of the prepared texts, active engagement in discussions directed by the lecturer and in response to peer questions/discussion, and participation in other class learning activities such as class exercises. Students should aim to participate in minimum of 85% of classes. Please keep in contact with the course coordinator if you are having difficulty.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
